nativeRange.habitat: Fern Finger inhabits shallow tropical marine and estuarine environments, typically found in mangrove lagoons, protected bays, coastal canals, and shallow reef flats at depths of 0 to 5 metres.

summary: Fern Finger is a lightly calcified green macroalgae from the Caribbean and Western Atlantic, producing upright fronds with whorled branches that give it a fern- or pipe-cleaner-like silhouette. It is generally considered more demanding than non-calcified macroalgae and tends to do best in reef tanks with stable alkalinity and calcium levels.
careNotes:
Fern Finger (Batophora sp.) is a lightly calcified green macroalgae in the family Dasycladaceae, closely related to Neomeris. It produces erect fronds with whorled lateral branches, giving individual thalli (the plant-like bodies of algae) a fern- or pipe-cleaner-like silhouette. In the wild, it inhabits shallow tropical waters of the Caribbean and Western Atlantic — including Florida, the Gulf of Mexico, Belize, Costa Rica, Panama, Venezuela, and Bermuda — typically in mangrove lagoons, protected bays, and shallow coastal flats.

As a calcareous (lightly calcium-reinforced) green alga, Batophora is generally considered more demanding than non-calcified macroalgae. Stable alkalinity (kH 8–12) and calcium levels (400–450 ppm) tend to support healthy growth; fluctuations in these parameters have been observed to cause tissue bleaching or melting. Medium lighting is generally recommended — excessive light may bleach the thallus, while insufficient light tends to result in slow or arrested growth. Growth rate is typically described as medium, slower than filamentous greens but faster than the most demanding calcareous forms such as Acetabularia or Penicillus.

In the aquarium trade, Batophora sp. is available under several common names including Fern Finger, Pipe-Cleaner Fern, and Hornwort Fern. The Pipe-Cleaner Fern form is described as fuzzier and more flexible than other variants. The genus currently has a limited presence in the hobby and has been found primarily through specialty macroalgae suppliers rather than mainstream reef retailers.

The most commonly referenced species in this genus for aquariums is B. oerstedii, also known in invasion biology literature as Fuzzy Finger Algae. A second species, B. occidentalis, is recognised but less documented in hobby contexts. The genus was established by J. Agardh in 1854.

Fern Finger is generally considered reef-safe and has been noted to be compatible with snails, hermit crabs, and most shrimp. Grazing fish and urchins may consume the thallus. It is typically sold as 1–1.5 inch cuttings on plug or rock.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
